top_weaknesses.issue: Several rhetorical filler/transition phrases read as AI-essay-template: 'Strip the abstraction off these numbers,' 'a pattern emerges,' 'This is the structural feature,' 'The forward question is not whether...'
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace each meta-transition with the actual next claim or a concrete example; cut the bridging sentence entirely where the data already speaks

top_weaknesses.issue: H2s are abstract essay-chapter titles ('What the data actually shows', 'What stops being possible') — they bury the core nouns (entry-level hiring, junior roles, career ladder) and miss SERP/skim alignment
top_weaknesses.fix: Rewrite H2s to carry concrete nouns, e.g. 'Entry-level postings have collapsed since 2023', 'What ends when the ladder ends'

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice flips from concrete journalist mode to policy-wonk macro register in the closing two sections (mobility quintiles, 40-year compounding)
top_weaknesses.fix: Either tone the closing back to the concrete metaphor (staircase/rungs) or land on a specific anecdote rather than a sweeping policy claim

top_weaknesses.issue: First-155-char snippet leads with narrow keyword ('computer science graduates') and truncates before the staircase/on-ramp payoff
top_weaknesses.fix: Restructure lede so the staircase line lands in the first 155 chars

consensus_summary: Strong contrarian title and core metaphor; the body weakens by drifting into essay-template transitions and abstract section titles. Sharpen H2s to carry concrete nouns, cut the meta-transitions, and this ships.
